nameMeanings.name: Cornhill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: locative name in SE England mainly from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumb) or one of the many minor places so called. It has often become   Cornell   and has sometimes been confused with   Cornwall  , and   Cornwell  , as in the case of Thomas Cornell, 1722 in   Parish Registers   (Horringer, Suffolk), who is also called Cornwall (1736), Cornwell (1740) and Cornhill (1766).

variants.name: Cornwall
meanings: habitational name from the county of Cornwall, which is named with the Old English tribal name Cornwealas. This is from Kernow (the term that the Cornish used to refer to themselves, a word of uncertain etymology, perhaps connected with a Celtic element meaning ‘horn, headland’), + Old English wealas ‘strangers, foreigners’, the term used by the Anglo-Saxons for British-speaking people.

variants.name: Parish Registers
meanings: English:  see  Lonsdale  .   Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorks Surnames   notes Tobias Londsdale or Lansdall, 1675–84 in   Parish Registers   (Keighley, WR Yorks).

variants.name: Cornwell
meanings: habitational name from Cornwell in Oxfordshire, named from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

variant of Cornwall or Cornhill; in southeastern England mainly a habitational name from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumberland) or one of the many minor places so called, often becoming Cornell .

variants.name: Cornell
meanings: English: shortened form of Cornwell , Cornwall , or of Cornhill, a habitational name from a place in Northumberland named Cornhill, from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + halh ‘nook, recess’; or from Cornhill in London, a medieval grain exchange, named with Old English corn ‘corn, grain’ + hyll ‘hill’, or from some other place elsewhere similarly named.
English: possibly also a nickname from French corneille ‘rook, crow’, probably denoting a chatterer or someone with dark hair or a dark complexion.

variants.name: Cornall
meanings: 1 English: the Lancs name is a variant of Corney , where -all and -ell are hypercorrect pronunciations of Cornah, Corner, and Cornoe, late pronunciations of Corney.

2 English: the Devon name is a variant of Cornwall . Here also probably belongs Gurnall, as in Joan Gurnall, 1586 in IGI (Crediton, Devon), and William Gurnall, 1650 in IGI (Lavenham, Suffolk). Compare Corney .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Cornhill was born about 1115, in Cornhill on Tweed, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, her father, Edward Cornhill, was 67 and her mother, Godeleve Southwark, was 69. She had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with Gervase de Cornhill.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Cornhill(이)가 대략 1115년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 코른힐  트위드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edward Cornhill(은)는 67세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Godeleve Southwark(은)는 69세였다. 그녀는 Gervase de Cornhill(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.
